lookup_context: Use the abbreviation table as a bidirectional lookup
items.task: Find the abbreviation paired with the meaning Approval Request.
items.instruction: Use TBL 1-2-1 as a lookup table.
items.question_text: In TBL 1-2-1, which abbreviation corresponds to Approval Request?
items.explanation: TBL 1-2-1 pairs APREQ with Approval Request.

items.task: Use the clearance abbreviation table to decode CAF.
items.question_text: In the 2-3-10 clearance abbreviation table, what does CAF mean?
items.explanation: In the 2-3-10 clearance abbreviation table, CAF means cleared as filed.

items.source_label: En route flight progress strip block table in 2-3-2
items.task: Find the block number assigned to requested altitude on the en route strip.
items.instruction: Use the en route strip block table.
items.question_text: On the 2-3-2 en route flight progress strip table, which block records requested altitude?
items.explanation: The en route strip table assigns requested altitude to block 24.

items.source_label: Oceanic strip reduced-separation flag table in 2-3-3
lookup_context: Meaning to locate: Reduced Vertical Separation Minimum
items.task: Find the flag letter paired with RVSM in the oceanic data entry table.
items.instruction: Use the oceanic strip reduced-separation flag table.
items.question_text: Which 2-3-3 oceanic strip flag indicates Reduced Vertical Separation Minimum?
items.explanation: The oceanic strip table uses W for RVSM.

items.source_label: Terminal arrival strip data-entry table in 2-3-4
lookup_context: Data: minimum fuel, destination airport, point out, radar vector, or speed adjustment
items.task: Identify the space assigned to that set of arrival-strip information.
items.instruction: Use the terminal arrival strip data-entry table.
items.question_text: On the 2-3-4 arrival strip table, which space may contain minimum fuel, destination airport, point out, radar vector, or speed adjustment information?
items.explanation: The terminal arrival strip table assigns that set of information to space 9A.

items.task: Use the correct row and column of the light-gun signal table.
items.question_text: In TBL 3-2-1, what does a steady green signal mean for an aircraft on the ground?

choices.text: Return to starting point on the airport.

items.explanation: TBL 3-2-1 shows that steady green means cleared for takeoff for an aircraft on the ground.

lookup_context: Recipient/status: vehicles, equipment, and personnel on the movement area
items.task: Use the ground-traffic column of the light-gun signal table.
items.question_text: In TBL 3-2-1, what does a flashing white signal mean for movement of vehicles, equipment, and personnel?

choices.text: Return to starting point on the airport.

items.explanation: For movement-area vehicles, equipment, and personnel, TBL 3-2-1 assigns flashing white the meaning return to starting point on the airport.

lookup_context: Visibility: 2 to but not including 3 miles
items.task: Select the intensity step matching the daytime visibility band.
items.question_text: Under TBL 3-4-8, what daytime setting applies when visibility is 2 to but not including 3 miles?
items.explanation: TBL 3-4-8 assigns step 3 for daytime visibility from 2 to but not including 3 miles.

lookup_context: Lighting system: HIRL associated with MALSR
items.task: Select the night setting for the visibility band.
items.question_text: Under TBL 3-4-9, what night setting applies when visibility is more than 3 miles?
items.explanation: TBL 3-4-9 assigns step 1 at night when visibility is more than 3 miles.

items.task: Select the MIRL setting matching the daytime visibility band.
items.question_text: Under TBL 3-4-10, what daytime MIRL setting applies when visibility is less than 2 miles?
items.explanation: TBL 3-4-10 assigns step 3 during the day when visibility is less than 2 miles.

items.task: Select the taxiway-light setting matching the night visibility band.
items.question_text: Under TBL 3-4-11, what night setting applies to a three-step taxiway-light system when visibility is less than 1 mile?
items.explanation: TBL 3-4-11 assigns step 2 at night when visibility is less than 1 mile.

items.task: Select the REIL setting matching the daytime visibility band.
items.question_text: Under TBL 3-4-1, what daytime setting applies to a three-step REIL system when visibility is 2 to 5 miles inclusive?

choices.text: Pilot request only; no standard daytime setting exists for that visibility range.

items.explanation: TBL 3-4-1 assigns setting 2 for daytime visibility of 2 to 5 miles inclusive.

items.task: Select the VASI intensity for the stated time condition.
items.question_text: Under TBL 3-4-3, what intensity applies to a three-step VASI system during twilight?
items.explanation: TBL 3-4-3 assigns the medium setting to twilight for a three-step VASI system.

lookup_context: Time: night from sunset to sunrise
items.task: Select the PAPI intensity for the stated time condition.
items.question_text: Under TBL 3-4-4, what intensity applies to a five-step PAPI system at night from sunset to sunrise?
items.explanation: TBL 3-4-4 assigns step 3 at night.

lookup_context: Visibility: 1 to but not including 3 miles
items.task: Select the ALS intensity step matching the daytime visibility band.
items.question_text: Under TBL 3-4-5, what daytime ALS intensity step applies when visibility is 1 to but not including 3 miles?
items.explanation: TBL 3-4-5 assigns step 4 during the day for visibility from 1 to but not including 3 miles.

lookup_context: Lighting system: three-step MALSR or ODALS
items.task: Select the MALSR/ODALS setting matching the daytime visibility band.
items.question_text: Under TBL 3-4-7, what daytime setting applies to a three-step MALSR or ODALS system when visibility is 2 to 5 miles inclusive?
items.explanation: TBL 3-4-7 assigns setting 2 for daytime visibility from 2 to 5 miles inclusive.

items.task: Select the required runway centerline spacing for the stated aircraft mix.
items.instruction: Use the same-direction runway spacing table.
items.question_text: Under TBL 3-8-1, if either aircraft is a Heavy, what minimum runway centerline spacing is required for simultaneous same-direction operations?
items.explanation: TBL 3-8-1 requires 700 feet between runway centerlines if either aircraft is a Heavy.

items.task: Select the runway centerline spacing for the stated time condition.
items.instruction: Use the opposite-direction runway spacing table.
items.question_text: Under TBL 3-8-2, what minimum runway centerline spacing is required for simultaneous opposite-direction operations between sunset and sunrise?
items.explanation: TBL 3-8-2 requires 2,800 feet between runway centerlines between sunset and sunrise.

items.task: Select the normal usable radius distance for the stated NAVAID class and altitude band.
items.question_text: Under TBL 4-1-1, what normal usable radius distance applies to an H-class VOR/VORTAC/TACAN from 18,000 feet through FL450?
items.explanation: TBL 4-1-1 gives 130 miles for H-class VOR/VORTAC/TACAN from 18,000 feet through FL450.

items.source_label: Cruising altitude/flight level assignment table in 4-5-2
items.task: Choose the flight level that matches the course band and altitude stratum.
items.question_text: Using the 4-5-2 altitude-assignment table, an aircraft on magnetic course 250 degrees is at the high-altitude stratum. Which assignment follows the table?
items.explanation: At and below FL410, courses from 180 through 359 degrees use even cardinal flight levels, so FL380 fits the table.

items.source_label: Lowest usable flight level table in 4-5-4
items.task: Locate the altimeter-setting band and return the corresponding lowest usable flight level.
items.question_text: Using the 4-5-4 table, what is the lowest usable flight level when the altimeter setting is 29.91?
items.explanation: An altimeter setting from 29.91 through 28.92 makes FL190 the lowest usable flight level.

items.source_label: Minimum flight level adjustment table in 4-5-5
items.task: Locate the altimeter-setting band and choose the required adjustment.
items.question_text: Using the 4-5-5 adjustment table, what adjustment factor applies when the altimeter setting is 29.55?
items.explanation: Altimeter settings from 29.91 through 29.42 require a 500-foot adjustment.

items.task: Find the release-distance stagger paired with the divergence and centerline-separation conditions.
items.question_text: Under TBL 5-8-1, if divergence occurs immediately after departure and minimum centerline separation is 700 feet, what release distance stagger applies?
items.explanation: TBL 5-8-1 pairs immediate divergence with 700 feet minimum centerline separation and a 1,000-foot release distance stagger.
